title: Block scalars — Story + AC checklist
description: Same task, prompt formatted as a user story with explicit acceptance criteria checklist.
prompt:
## Story

As a consumer of `parseYamlSubset`, I need support for YAML block scalars (`|` and `>`) so that multiline content can be expressed naturally in artifact config files.

The parser is in `packages/ai-artifacts/lib.js`. Do not use external YAML libraries.

## Context

- `|` is a literal block scalar: preserves newlines exactly as written.
- `>` is a folded block scalar: joins consecutive lines with a space, but double newlines become paragraph breaks.
- Block content indentation is determined by the first non-empty line after the indicator.
- A single trailing newline is always appended.
- Strip/keep/clip modifiers (`|-`, `|+`) are NOT required.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `key: |\n  line1\n  line2\n` parses to `{ key: "line1\nline2\n" }`
- [ ] `key: >\n  line1\n  line2\n` parses to `{ key: "line1 line2\n" }`
- [ ] Folded mode: a blank line between content lines produces a paragraph break (`\n\n`)
- [ ] A block scalar followed by a sibling key at the same indentation level parses both keys correctly
- [ ] Block scalars work as items inside a sequence (`- |\n    content`)
- [ ] Lines indented further than the minimum are preserved with their extra indentation (literal mode)
- [ ] All existing tests pass: `npm run test:ai-artifacts`
- [ ] A new test file `packages/ai-artifacts/lib.block-scalars.test.js` exists with at least 6 test cases
- [ ] No external dependencies added to `packages/ai-artifacts/package.json`

## Definition of Done

All acceptance criteria above are met. Tests pass. No regressions.

acceptance_criteria.description: Literal block scalar preserves newlines in a simple mapping
acceptance_criteria.command:
node -e "const {parseArtifactConfig}=require('./packages/ai-artifacts/lib.js'); const r=parseArtifactConfig('key: |
  line1
  line2
'); if(r.key !== 'line1
line2
') { console.error('expected line1\nline2\n, got:', JSON.stringify(r.key)); process.exit(1) }"

acceptance_criteria.description: Folded block scalar folds single newlines to spaces
acceptance_criteria.command:
node -e "const {parseArtifactConfig}=require('./packages/ai-artifacts/lib.js'); const r=parseArtifactConfig('key: >
  line1
  line2
'); if(r.key !== 'line1 line2
') { console.error('expected line1 line2\n, got:', JSON.stringify(r.key)); process.exit(1) }"

acceptance_criteria.description: Folded block scalar preserves double newlines as paragraph breaks
acceptance_criteria.command:
node -e "const {parseArtifactConfig}=require('./packages/ai-artifacts/lib.js'); const r=parseArtifactConfig('key: >
  para1

  para2
'); if(r.key !== 'para1

para2
') { console.error('expected para1\n\npara2\n, got:', JSON.stringify(r.key)); process.exit(1) }"

acceptance_criteria.description: Block scalar followed by another key at same level parses both correctly
acceptance_criteria.command:
node -e "const {parseArtifactConfig}=require('./packages/ai-artifacts/lib.js'); const r=parseArtifactConfig('desc: |
  hello
  world
name: foo
'); if(r.desc !== 'hello
world
' || r.name !== 'foo') { console.error('got:', JSON.stringify(r)); process.exit(1) }"

acceptance_criteria.description: Block scalar works as a sequence item
acceptance_criteria.command:
node -e "const {parseArtifactConfig}=require('./packages/ai-artifacts/lib.js'); const r=parseArtifactConfig('items:
  - |
    line1
    line2
  - plain
'); if(!Array.isArray(r.items) || r.items[0] !== 'line1
line2
' || r.items[1] !== 'plain') { console.error('got:', JSON.stringify(r)); process.exit(1) }"

acceptance_criteria.description: Literal block scalar preserves lines indented beyond minimum
acceptance_criteria.command:
node -e "const {parseArtifactConfig}=require('./packages/ai-artifacts/lib.js'); const r=parseArtifactConfig('code: |
  if true:
    do_thing
'); if(r.code !== 'if true:
  do_thing
') { console.error('got:', JSON.stringify(r.code)); process.exit(1) }"

acceptance_criteria.description: No regressions in existing test suite
Agent created a dedicated test file for block scalars
No new dependencies added to package.json
